summary: First-iteration backend regression for Helix Platform. 25/25 pytest tests pass against live preview URL. Covered: service metadata, JWT register/login/me, role-based access (401/403), FX, full Catalog CRUD with auto-NGN price, Businesses + KYB submission + admin approval (provisions Anchor accounts), full Order lifecycle (RFQ -> proforma idempotent 400 on re-submit -> simulate-payment -> status advancement) + all 4 PDFs (proforma/commercial/packing/origin) returning real PDF bytes, Compliance docs/score/requirements, Finance dashboard/transactions/withdraw (insufficient-balance guard) + admin overview, Anchor webhook accepting mocked event with skipped sig verification.

minor.issue: create_business_customer / create_individual_customer / submit_kyb / create_deposit_account in onboarding.py are called synchronously in async handlers. They are mock no-op stubs today so it works, but if Anchor mock is swapped for real HTTP later, this will block the event loop. Recommend awaiting via asyncio.to_thread or making the client async.
initiate_nip_transfer is sync inside async handler — same as above.
response_model=Business | None — pydantic union with None response is fine but for buyers with no business it returns 200 + null; consider 204 for clarity.
Uses synchronous `requests.get` inside an async route — should be httpx.AsyncClient to avoid blocking the event loop.

action_items: Optional: replace blocking `requests.get` in /api/auth/emergent/session with an async HTTP client.

Optional: wrap synchronous Anchor-client calls (create_*_customer, submit_kyb, create_deposit_account, initiate_nip_transfer) with asyncio.to_thread or convert to async — important before flipping ANCHOR_ENV to a real env.

Cosmetic: when seed already approved both businesses, /api/admin/verifications can return [] — that is acceptable per spec; a manual KYB flow exercised in test_business_creation_kyb_and_admin_decide proves the queue + decide path.
Idempotency on POST /api/orders/{oid}/simulate-payment is correct (returns {status: already_confirmed} on retry) and POST /api/orders/{oid}/proforma correctly returns 400 if already confirmed.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: clean bcrypt + JWT + session-token resolution (cookie OR bearer). Good pattern.
All Mongo queries explicitly project {_id: 0} — no ObjectId leakage observed in any tested response.
PDFs use ReportLab and return Response(media_type='application/pdf') with %PDF magic bytes verified.
FX endpoint returns live usd_to_ngn (saw 1485 at test time) with a sane fallback (1650).
Role enforcement: require_roles dependency works (buyer hitting /api/admin/verifications returns 403; unauthenticated returns 401).
Webhook in mock mode bypasses signature verification — confirmed; logged as informational.
Anchor sandbox_mock returns deterministic ids (anchor_cust_*, anchor_acct_*, nip_*) — endpoints accept and persist them.
Minor: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') — FastAPI lifespan handler is preferred but functional.
test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md: admin@helix.com / exporter@helix.com / buyer@helix.com all with Helix@123. Seed data verified: 6 products, 2 orders, 4 compliance docs, 2 approved businesses, exporter pre-credited ~15600 USD (-156 fee = 15444 net) and ~8.4M NGN.
seed_data_creation: No new seed needed; tests create TEST_-prefixed transient data (products, businesses, compliance docs, orders) and clean up products. Test data left in DB: a few transient TEST_ businesses/orders that don't affect future runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid. For next iteration (frontend), expect REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://trade-nexus-110.preview.emergentagent.com. Login flows: JWT email/password works; Emergent OAuth uses /api/auth/emergent/session with session_id then sets httpOnly cookie. The simulate-payment endpoint is the manual trigger replacing a real Anchor account.credited webhook — frontend should expose a 'Mark Payment Received' button on confirmed orders that calls POST /api/orders/{oid}/simulate-payment. Transactions accumulate per-test-run; if you need clean balances drop the `transactions` collection or re-seed.
